fields.name: The Forest
fields.description: deep dive into forest dungeons to find a lost civilization

fields.name: Acid
fields.description: Corrosive damage that dissolves materials

fields.name: Bludgeoning
fields.description: Blunt force trauma from impacts

fields.name: Cold
fields.description: Freezing damage from ice and cold

fields.name: Fire
fields.description: Burning damage from flames and heat

fields.name: Force
fields.description: Pure magical energy damage

fields.name: Lightning
fields.description: Electrical damage from lightning and electricity

fields.name: Necrotic
fields.description: Death magic that withers living tissue

fields.name: Piercing
fields.description: Sharp pointed damage from spears, arrows, etc.

fields.name: Poison
fields.description: Toxic damage from venoms and toxins

fields.name: Psychic
fields.description: Mental damage affecting the mind

fields.name: Radiant
fields.description: Divine light energy damage

fields.name: Slashing
fields.description: Cutting damage from blades and claws

fields.name: Thunder
fields.description: Sonic boom damage from loud sounds

fields.name: Blinded
fields.description: Cannot see; attacks have disadvantage, others have advantage

fields.name: Charmed
fields.description: Cannot attack the charmer; charmer has advantage on social interactions

fields.name: Deafened
fields.description: Cannot hear; automatically fails hearing-based ability checks

fields.name: Frightened
fields.description: Disadvantage on ability checks and attacks while source is in sight

fields.name: Grappled
fields.description: Speed becomes 0; ends if grappler is incapacitated

fields.name: Incapacitated
fields.description: Cannot take actions or reactions

fields.name: Invisible
fields.description: Cannot be seen; attacks have advantage, others have disadvantage

fields.name: Paralyzed
fields.description: Incapacitated and cannot move or speak; auto-fails Str and Dex saves

fields.name: Petrified
fields.description: Transformed into stone; incapacitated and unaware

fields.name: Poisoned
fields.description: Disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks

fields.name: Prone
fields.description: Can only crawl; disadvantage on attacks; others have advantage on melee

fields.name: Restrained
fields.description: Speed becomes 0; disadvantage on attacks and Dex saves

fields.name: Stunned
fields.description: Incapacitated and cannot move; auto-fails Str and Dex saves

fields.name: Unconscious
fields.description: Incapacitated, cannot move/speak, unaware; prone; auto-fails saves

fields.name: Acrobatics
fields.description: Balance, tumbling, and aerial maneuvers

fields.name: Animal Handling
fields.description: Calming and controlling animals

fields.name: Arcana
fields.description: Knowledge of magic, magical theory, and spells

fields.name: Athletics
fields.description: Physical activities like climbing and swimming

fields.name: Deception
fields.description: Lying and misleading others

fields.name: History
fields.description: Knowledge of historical events and figures

fields.name: Insight
fields.description: Reading people and understanding motivations

fields.name: Intimidation
fields.description: Threatening and coercing others

fields.name: Investigation
fields.description: Logical deduction and finding clues

fields.name: Medicine
fields.description: Treating wounds and diagnosing illnesses

fields.name: Nature
fields.description: Knowledge of natural phenomena and creatures

fields.name: Perception
fields.description: Noticing things with your senses

fields.name: Performance
fields.description: Entertaining others through various arts

fields.name: Persuasion
fields.description: Convincing others through reason and charm

fields.name: Religion
fields.description: Knowledge of deities, religions, and divine magic

fields.name: Sleight of Hand
fields.description: Manual dexterity and pickpocketing

fields.name: Stealth
fields.description: Moving unseen and unheard

fields.name: Survival
fields.description: Tracking, navigation, and wilderness survival

fields.name: Human
fields.description: Versatile and ambitious

fields.name: Elf
fields.description: Graceful and magical

fields.name: Dwarf
fields.description: Hardy and determined

fields.name: Halfling
fields.description: Small and brave

fields.name: Dragonborn
fields.description: Draconic heritage

fields.name: Gnome
fields.description: Small and clever

fields.name: Goliath
fields.description: Strong mountain dwellers

fields.name: Orc
fields.description: Strong and fierce

fields.name: Tiefling
fields.description: Fiendish heritage

fields.name: Acolyte
fields.description: You devoted yourself to service in a temple, either nestled in a town or secluded in a sacred grove. There you performed rites in honor of a god or pantheon. You served under a priest and studied religion. Thanks to your priest's instruction and your own devotion, you also learned how to channel a modicum of divine power in service to your place of worship and the people who prayed there.

fields.name: Criminal
fields.description: You eked out a living in dark alleyways, cutting purses or burgling shops. Perhaps you were part of a small gang of like-minded wrongdoers who looked out for each other. Or maybe you were a lone wolf, fending for yourself against the local thieves' guild and more fearsome lawbreakers.

fields.name: Sage
fields.description: You spent your formative years traveling between manors and monasteries, performing various odd jobs and services in exchange for access to their libraries. You whiled away many a long evening studying books and scrolls, learning the lore of the multiverse—even the rudiments of magic—and your mind yearns for more.

fields.name: Soldier
fields.description: You began training for war as soon as you reached adulthood and carry precious few memories of life before you took up arms. Battle is in your blood. Sometimes you catch yourself reflexively performing the basic fighting exercises you learned first. Eventually, you put that training to use on the battlefield, protecting the realm by waging war.

fields.name: Barbarian
fields.description: Barbarians are mighty warriors who are powered by primal forces of the multiverse that manifest as a Rage. More than a mere emotion—and not limited to anger—this Rage is an incarnation of a predator's ferocity, a storm's fury, and a sea's turmoil. Some Barbarians personify their Rage as a fierce spirit or revered forebear. Others see it as a connection to the pain and anguish of the world, as an impersonal tangle of wild magic, or as an expression of their own deepest self. For every Barbarian, their Rage is a power that fuels not just battle prowess, but also uncanny reflexes and heightened senses.

fields.name: Bard
fields.description: Bards are masters of song, speech, and the magic they contain. You have learned to untangle and reshape the fabric of reality in harmony with your wishes and music. The multiverse reverberates with a grand music, and Bards are attuned to it in a way that lets them reshape the world. In the worlds of D&D, words and music are not just vibrations of air, but vocalizations with power all their own. The Bard is a master of the spoken word and instrumental music, channeling power through performance. Bards say that the cosmos is a work of art, for it was spoken into existence. The gods' own words gave it shape, and echoes of these primordial Words of Creation still resound throughout the cosmos. The music of creation still resounds throughout the multiverse, a fundamental magic called the Song of Creation. The cosmos is a work of art, for it was sung and spoken into existence.
fields.weapon_proficiencies: ["Simple weapons", "Martial weapons that have the Finesse or Light property"]

fields.name: Cleric
fields.description: Clerics draw power from the realms of the gods and harness it to work miracles. Blessed by a deity, a pantheon, or another immortal entity, a Cleric can reach out to the divine magic of the Outer Planes—where gods dwell—and channel it to bolster people and battle foes. Because their power is a divine gift, Clerics typically associate themselves with temples dedicated to the deity or other immortal force that unlocked their magic. Harnessing divine magic doesn't rely on specific training, yet Clerics might learn prayers and rites that help them draw on power from the Outer Planes.

fields.name: Druid
fields.description: Druids revere nature above all, gaining their spells and other magical powers either from the force of nature itself or from a nature deity. Many druids pursue a mystic spirituality of transcendent union with nature rather than devotion to a divine entity, while others serve gods of wild nature, animals, or elemental forces. The ancient druidic traditions are sometimes called the Old Faith, in contrast to the worship of gods in temples and shrines. Druid spells are oriented toward nature and animals—the power of tooth and claw, of sun and moon, of fire and storm. Druids also gain the ability to take on animal forms, and some druids make a particular study of this practice, even to the point where they prefer animal form to their natural form.
fields.weapon_proficiencies: ["Simple weapons", "Martial weapons that have the Light or Finesse property"]

fields.name: Fighter
fields.description: Fighters rule many battlefields. Questing knights, royal champions, elite soldiers, and hardened mercenaries—as Fighters, they all share an unparalleled prowess with weapons and armor. And they are well acquainted with death, both meting it out and defying it. Fighters master various weapon techniques, and a well-equipped Fighter always has the right tool at hand for any combat situation. Likewise, a Fighter is adept with every form of armor. Beyond that basic degree of familiarity, each Fighter specializes in certain styles of combat. Some concentrate on archery, some on fighting with two weapons at once, and some on augmenting their martial skills with magic. This combination of broad ability and extensive specialization makes Fighters superior combatants.

fields.name: Monk
fields.description: Monks use rigorous combat training and mental discipline to align themselves with the multiverse and focus their internal reservoirs of power. Different Monks conceptualize this power in various ways: as breath, energy, life force, essence, or self, for example. Whether channeled as a striking display of martial prowess or as a subtler focus of defensive ability and speed, this power infuses all of a Monk's abilities. Monks harness and focus their internal power to create extraordinary, even supernatural, effects. They channel uncanny speed and strength into their attacks, with or without the use of weapons. As they gain experience, their martial training and their mastery of their internal power make them more formidable with each passing day. A Monk's internal power is called ki in some traditions, but it has many names, such as chi, od, prana, and pneuma.
fields.weapon_proficiencies: ["Simple weapons", "Martial weapons that have the Light property"]
fields.tool_proficiencies: ["One type of Artisan's Tools or one Musical Instrument"]

fields.name: Paladin
fields.description: Paladins are united by their oaths to stand against the forces of annihilation and corruption. Whether sworn before a god's altar, in a sacred glade before nature spirits and Fey witnesses, or in a moment of desperation and grief with the dead as the only witnesses, a Paladin's oath is a powerful bond. It is a source of power that turns a devout warrior into a blessed champion. A Paladin swears to stand against corrupting influences and to hunt the forces of rampant destruction wherever they lurk. Different Paladins focus on various aspects of these causes, but all are bound by the oaths that grant them power to do their sacred work. Although many Paladins are devoted to gods of good, a Paladin's power comes from a commitment to justice itself, not from a god.

fields.name: Ranger
fields.description: Rangers are warriors of the wilderness, specializing in tracking, survival, and combat. They are skilled in fighting the enemies that threaten the edges of civilization—humanoid raiders, rampaging beasts and monstrosities, terrible giants, and deadly dragons. Rangers learn to track their quarry as a predator does, moving stealthily through the wilds and hiding themselves in brush and rubble. Thanks to their connection with nature, Rangers can cast spells that harness primal forces to aid them in their pursuits. A Ranger's talents and abilities are honed with deadly focus on the grim task of protecting the borderlands and the people who live there.

fields.name: Rogue
fields.description: Rogues rely on cunning, stealth, and their foes' vulnerabilities to get the upper hand in any situation. They have a knack for finding the solution to just about any problem, bringing resourcefulness and versatility to their adventuring parties. Rogues devote as much effort to mastering the use of a variety of skills as they do to perfecting their combat abilities, giving them broad capabilities that few other characters can match. Some Rogues who turn to adventuring are former criminals who have decided that dodging monsters is preferable to dodging the law. Others are professional killers in search of a profitable application of their talents between contracts. Some have taken up thievery as a means of acquiring the coin needed to pay for their family's needs. Others are investigators, diplomats, or troubleshooters.
fields.weapon_proficiencies: ["Simple weapons", "Martial weapons that have the Finesse or Light property"]

fields.name: Sorcerer
fields.description: Sorcerers have a magical birthright conferred upon them by an exotic bloodline, some otherworldly influence, or exposure to unknown cosmic forces. No one chooses sorcery; the power chooses the sorcerer. Sorcerers have no use for spellbooks or the ancient knowledge of magic lore. They learn to harness their power, to channel it rather than being consumed by it. The appearance of sorcerous powers is wildly unpredictable. Some draconic bloodlines produce exactly one sorcerer in every generation, but in other lines of descent every individual is a sorcerer. Most of the time, the talents of sorcery appear as apparent flukes. Some sorcerers can't name the origin of their power, while others trace it to strange events in their own lives. The touch of a demon, the blessing of a dryad at a baby's birth, or a taste of the water from a mysterious spring might spark the gift of sorcery.

fields.name: Warlock
fields.description: Warlocks quest for knowledge that lies hidden in the fabric of the multiverse. They often begin their search for magical power by delving into tomes of forbidden lore, dabbling in invocations meant to attract the power of extraplanar beings, or seeking places of power where the influence of these beings can be felt. In no time, each Warlock is drawn into a binding pact with an extraplanar being. Drawing on the alien knowledge of their patrons, Warlocks piece together arcane secrets to bolster their own power. Unlike Wizards or Sorcerers, Warlocks don't rely on their own innate magical abilities. Instead, they are the servants—and sometimes the masters—of beings that exist beyond the veil of known reality. The beings that serve as patrons for Warlocks are mighty inhabitants of other planes of existence—not gods, but almost godlike in their power.

fields.name: Wizard
fields.description: Wizards are defined by their exhaustive study of magic's inner workings. They cast spells of explosive fire, arcing lightning, subtle deception, and spectacular transformations. Their magic conjures monsters from other planes of existence, glimpses the future, or forms protective barriers. Their mightiest spells change one substance into another, call meteors from the sky, or open portals to other worlds. Most Wizards share a scholarly approach to magic. They examine the theoretical underpinnings of magic, particularly the categorization of spells into schools of magic.

fields.name: Ability Score Improvement
fields.benefits: [{"name": "Ability Score Increase", "description": "Increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. This feat can't increase an ability score above 20.", "type": "abilityScoreIncrease"}]

fields.name: Alert
fields.benefits: [{"name": "Initiative Proficiency", "description": "When you roll Initiative, you can add your Proficiency Bonus to the roll.", "type": "combat"}, {"name": "Initiative Swap", "description": "Immediately after you roll Initiative, you can swap your Initiative with the Initiative of one willing ally in the same combat. You can't make this swap if you or the ally has the Incapacitated condition.", "type": "combat"}]

fields.name: Archery
fields.benefits: [{"name": "Ranged Attack Bonus", "description": "You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls you make with Ranged weapons.", "type": "combat"}]

fields.name: Boon of Combat Prowess
fields.benefits: [{"name": "Ability Score Increase", "description": "Increase one ability score of your choice by 1, to a maximum of 30.", "type": "abilityScoreIncrease"}, {"name": "Peerless Aim", "description": "When you miss with an attack roll, you can hit instead. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until the start of your next turn.", "type": "combat"}]

fields.name: Boon of Dimensional Travel
fields.benefits: [{"name": "Ability Score Increase", "description": "Increase your Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma score by 1, to a maximum of 30.", "type": "abilityScoreIncrease"}, {"name": "Misty Step", "description": "As a Bonus Action, you can cast Misty Step without expending a spell slot. Once you cast the spell in this way, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest, unless you expend a level 2 spell slot to cast it again.", "type": "spellcasting"}]

fields.name: Boon of Fate
fields.benefits: [{"name": "Ability Score Increase", "description": "Increase your Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma score by 1, to a maximum of 30.", "type": "abilityScoreIncrease"}, {"name": "Twist of Fate", "description": "When you or another creature within 60 feet of you makes a D20 Test, you can use your Reaction to cause the creature to reroll, and the creature must use the new roll. Once you use this Reaction, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest.", "type": "special"}]

fields.name: Boon of Irresistible Offense
fields.benefits: [{"name": "Ability Score Increase", "description": "Increase your Strength, Dexterity, or Constitution score by 1, to a maximum of 30.", "type": "abilityScoreIncrease"}, {"name": "Overwhelming Strike", "description": "When you deal damage with a weapon or an Unarmed Strike, you can change the damage type to Force, and you can push the target up to 10 feet away from you. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you roll Initiative or finish a Short or Long Rest.", "type": "attack"}]

fields.name: Boon of Night Spirit
fields.benefits: [{"name": "Ability Score Increase", "description": "Increase your Dexterity, Wisdom, or Charisma score by 1, to a maximum of 30.", "type": "abilityScoreIncrease"}, {"name": "Merge with Shadows", "description": "While entirely in Dim Light or Darkness, you have the Invisible condition. You aren't Invisible if you take an action, make an attack, or cast a spell.", "type": "special"}]

fields.name: Boon of Spell Recall
fields.benefits: [{"name": "Ability Score Increase", "description": "Increase your Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma score by 1, to a maximum of 30.", "type": "abilityScoreIncrease"}, {"name": "Free Casting", "description": "Whenever you cast a spell with a level 1\u20134 spell slot, roll 1d4. If the number you roll is the same as the slot's level, the slot isn't expended.", "type": "spellcasting"}]

fields.name: Boon of Truesight
fields.benefits: [{"name": "Ability Score Increase", "description": "Increase your Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma score by 1, to a maximum of 30.", "type": "abilityScoreIncrease"}, {"name": "True Seeing", "description": "You have Truesight with a range of 60 feet.", "type": "senses"}]

fields.name: Defense
fields.benefits: [{"name": "Armor Class Bonus", "description": "While you're wearing Light, Medium, or Heavy armor, you gain a +1 bonus to Armor Class.", "type": "combat"}]

fields.name: Grappler
fields.benefits: [{"name": "Ability Score Increase", "description": "Increase your Strength or Dexterity score by 1, to a maximum of 20.", "type": "abilityScoreIncrease"}, {"name": "Punch and Grab", "description": "When you hit a creature with an Unarmed Strike as part of the Attack action on your turn, you can use both the Damage and the Grapple option. You can use this benefit only once per turn.", "type": "combat"}, {"name": "Attack Advantage", "description": "You have Advantage on attack rolls against a creature Grappled by you.", "type": "combat"}, {"name": "Fast Wrestler", "description": "You don't have to spend extra movement to move a creature Grappled by you if the creature is your size or smaller.", "type": "utility"}]

fields.name: Great Weapon Fighting
fields.benefits: [{"name": "Enhanced Two-Handed Damage", "description": "When you roll damage for an attack you make with a Melee weapon that you are holding with two hands, you can treat any 1 or 2 on a damage die as a 3. The weapon must have the Two-Handed or Versatile property to gain this benefit.", "type": "combat"}]

fields.name: Magic Initiate
fields.benefits: [{"name": "Two Cantrips", "description": "You learn two cantrips of your choice from the Cleric, Druid, or Wizard spell list. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this feat's spells (choose when you select this feat).", "type": "spellcasting"}, {"name": "Level 1 Spell", "description": "Choose a level 1 spell from the same list you selected for this feat's cantrips. You always have that spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a Long Rest. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have.", "type": "spellcasting"}, {"name": "Spell Change", "description": "Whenever you gain a new level, you can replace one of the spells you chose for this feat with a different spell of the same level from the chosen spell list.", "type": "utility"}]

fields.name: Savage Attacker
fields.benefits: [{"name": "Enhanced Weapon Damage", "description": "Once per turn when you hit a target with a weapon, you can roll the weapon's damage dice twice and use either roll against the target.", "type": "combat"}]

fields.name: Skilled
fields.benefits: [{"name": "Skill and Tool Proficiencies", "description": "You gain proficiency in any combination of three skills or tools of your choice.", "type": "proficiency"}]

fields.name: Two-Weapon Fighting
fields.benefits: [{"name": "Off-Hand Damage Modifier", "description": "When you make an extra attack as a result of using a weapon that has the Light property, you can add your ability modifier to the damage of that attack if you aren't already adding it to the damage.", "type": "combat"}]
